title: Ctrl+C doesn't kill the container using `podman attach` with podman machine
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Cannot stop container attached by `podman attach` by pressing Ctrl+C

Similar to #14707 but for `podman attach` command

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine start`

2. `podman run --rm -d --name test nginx`

3. `podman attach test`

4. press `ctrl+c`

5. `podman ps --noheading`

**Describe the results you received:**

```txt
6b6c34680a8f  docker.io/library/nginx:latest  nginx -g daemon o...  9 seconds ago  Up 9 seconds ago              test
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```txt
(empty output)
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

It works as expected if `2.` and `3.` are replaced by `podman run --rm -it nginx`.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```txt

Version:      4.3.1
```

comments.body: Testing this locally on Linux, it seems to work correctly.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ashley-cui could you confirm this works for you on a Mac?

Seems like a legitimate issue.
```
acui@Ashleys-Mini podman % podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS             PORTS       NAMES
e91e0a763bf8  docker.io/library/nginx:latest  nginx -g daemon o...  17 seconds ago  Up 17 seconds ago              test
```
Might be a podman -remote versus podman issue?
Yep, looks like we have to plumb sig-proxy through the attach code as well. I'll give this a stab.
